commitLog.message: chore: add git hooks to enforce test gates on commit and push
chore: add git hooks to enforce test gates on commit and push
fix: resolve 6 high-severity findings from security audit #3

phase1.name: MVP e Validação de Fluxo
phase1.description: Core backend, AI qualification engine, and minimal chat frontend — the full end-to-end BANT flow running in production.

steps.name: Project Setup & Infrastructure
steps.description: Monorepo structure, Docker Compose stack, environment config, database init script, Makefile automation.

subTasks.name: Monorepo directory structure (maestro, ai-worker, frontend)
Docker Compose — postgres, redis, qdrant, minio, maestro, ai-worker, frontend
PostgreSQL init script with RLS, extensions, seed data
Makefile with 72 targets (dev, test, build, lint, deploy)
.env.example with all env vars documented
Optional monitoring stack (Prometheus + Grafana via profile)
Postman API collections (3 collections)

steps.name: Maestro — Domain Layer
steps.description: Go domain entities (Lead, Conversation, Message, Tenant, User), validation helpers, custom error types, repository port interfaces.

subTasks.name: Lead entity with BANT score, status enum, Validate()
Conversation entity with status machine, Validate()
Message entity with role enum, content-length cap, Validate()
Tenant entity with config JSONB, domain normalisation, Validate()
User entity with role enum
Custom error types: ErrNotFound, ErrValidation, ErrForbidden, ErrConflict
Repository port interfaces (ILeadRepository, IConversationRepository, IMessageRepository, ITenantRepository, IUserRepository)
Permission type + role→permission map (LeadsRead/Write, ConversationsRead/Write)

steps.name: Maestro — Infrastructure & Repository Layer
steps.description: PostgreSQL connection with retry, GORM-based repositories, tenant context isolation, database migrations.

subTasks.name: PostgreSQL connection with 5-attempt linear backoff retry
Connection pool tuning (max open/idle, ConnMaxLifetime, ConnMaxIdleTime)
Schema migrations (17 migration files incl. tenant domain/slug, RLS fixes, conversation status check)
LeadRepository — CRUD with tenant isolation, pagination cap, UpdateQualification split
ConversationRepository — CRUD with cross-tenant LeadID ownership check, page cap
MessageRepository — CRUD with 100-message cap, IDOR guards on read and insert
TenantRepository — CRUD with unique-constraint conflict mapping
UserRepository — CRUD with bcrypt password hashing
tenantIDFromContext returning ErrForbidden on missing tenant
Redis session store (JWT blacklist) + conversation history cache
DocumentRepository with tenant isolation (tenant_id scoped FindByID, FindAll, Delete, UpdateStatus)

steps.name: Maestro — HTTP Server & Security Middleware
steps.description: Gin HTTP server with timeouts, rate limiting, body size cap, JWT auth, CORS, security headers, RBAC, Prometheus metrics, and structured logging.

subTasks.name: Gin server with read/write/idle timeouts (Slowloris protection)
Token-bucket rate limiter (per-IP, RWMutex, 100K cap, auto-eviction)
X-Forwarded-For spoofing protection — CIDR width validation on TrustedProxies
Request body size middleware (1 MB default, configurable)
Custom request logger with sensitive param redaction (case-insensitive)
JWT authentication middleware with role enum validation, JTI, and optional blacklist check
CORS middleware via gin-contrib/cors with allowed origins
Security headers middleware (X-Frame-Options, HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy)
RBAC middleware — RequirePermission(p) and RequireOwner() with role→permission map
Request-ID middleware — X-Request-ID propagated through context and response header
Prometheus metrics endpoint (/metrics) with request count, duration, and status histograms
Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM
Health check endpoint (no env disclosure)
Config validation on startup (JWT_SECRET min 32 chars, APP_ENV enum, port range, AI_WORKER_URL)
WebSocket-specific rate limiter (3 RPS, burst 10 per IP) with per-conversation connection cap

steps.name: AI Worker — Core Engine
steps.description: Python FastAPI service with Groq LLM integration, BANT scoring, conversation history, streaming SSE.

subTasks.name: FastAPI application with middleware and route registration
Groq LLM client with streaming SSE responses
BANT scoring algorithm (Budget, Authority, Need, Timeline)
Prompt templates for BANT qualification agent
Redis-based conversation history management (atomic RPUSH/LRANGE)
Pydantic request/response schema validation
Health check endpoint
CORS origins list parsing (cors_origins_list method — production crash fixed)

steps.name: AI Worker — Security & SOLID Refactor
steps.description: JWT authentication middleware, per-tenant rate limiting, SOLID principles applied across all layers.

subTasks.name: JWT token verification middleware (pyjwt, HS256 algorithm pinning)
Tenant context extraction from JWT claims
Per-tenant rate limiting middleware (Redis INCR, TTL window)
Dependency injection for LLM client and scorer
Service layer separation (ChatService, ScorerService)
Structured logging with structlog
Full multi-provider fallback (Groq → OpenAI → Anthropic)
subTasks.notes: Descoped to Phase 2 — Groq as sole provider is sufficient for Phase 1 MVP

steps.name: Maestro — HTTP API Handlers (Leads & Conversations)
steps.description: HTTP handler implementations for the lead and conversation REST endpoints with RBAC.

subTasks.name: POST /api/v1/leads — create lead (LeadsWrite permission)
GET /api/v1/leads — list with filters and pagination (LeadsRead permission)
GET /api/v1/leads/:id — get lead by ID (LeadsRead permission)
PATCH /api/v1/leads/:id — update lead, RBAC-gated via LeadsWrite
DELETE /api/v1/leads/:id — hard-delete lead via LeadsWrite
POST /api/v1/conversations — start conversation (ConversationsWrite)
POST /api/v1/conversations/sandbox — create throw-away sandbox lead + conversation (ConversationsWrite)
GET /api/v1/conversations/:id — get conversation with full message history
DELETE /api/v1/conversations/sandbox/:id — delete sandbox conversation
POST /api/v1/auth/logout — blacklist JWT in Redis

steps.name: Maestro — Authentication (Signup, Login, JWT Issuance)
steps.description: User login, JWT access/refresh token issuance with JTI, refresh token rotation, bcrypt, logout blacklist, and RBAC enforcement.

subTasks.name: POST /api/v1/tenants/signup — public tenant + owner registration endpoint
POST /api/v1/auth/login — credential check + JWT issuance (accepts slug or UUID)
POST /api/v1/auth/refresh — refresh token rotation with new JTI
POST /api/v1/auth/logout — JTI blacklisted in Redis
Auth middleware (JWT validation, role enum check, tenant context injection, blacklist check)
Password hashing with bcrypt
RBAC — Permission-based middleware (LeadsRead/Write, ConversationsRead/Write, UsersManage, ConfigManage)
Structured audit logging on login success, failure, and refresh failure
Phase 2 roles (RoleOwner, RoleMember, RoleReadOnly) added to jwtutil.Parse() switch

steps.name: Maestro ↔ AI Worker Integration & WebSocket Streaming
steps.description: HTTP client from Maestro to AI Worker, WebSocket upgrade for real-time chat, SSE streaming proxy.

subTasks.name: HTTP client from Maestro to AI Worker with explicit timeout (resolves F-18)
Exponential backoff retry wrapper (max 3 attempts before stream starts)
SSE parser — content delta, [DONE], score event, error event
WebSocket upgrade endpoint GET /ws/chat/:conversation_id
SSE stream forwarding from AI Worker → WebSocket delta frames
[DONE] frame sent to client after AI stream ends — re-enables frontend input
Hub — thread-safe connection registry with 5-connection cap per conversation
Lead score update via LeadUseCase.UpdateScore after stream completes (decoupled from WS lifecycle)
WebSocket ConversationsWrite permission check applied
WS reconnect logic (handled by frontend ws-client.ts)

steps.name: RAG Document Pipeline
steps.description: Document chunking, embedding generation, vector retrieval, and context injection into LLM prompts.

subTasks.name: POST /api/v1/rag/corpus — file upload to MinIO with async indexing to Qdrant
GET /api/v1/rag/corpus — list documents (tenant-scoped)
DELETE /api/v1/rag/corpus/:id — delete document from MinIO + Qdrant
POST /api/v1/rag/corpus/:id/reindex — trigger re-embedding
Async chunking worker (LangChain RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter)
Embedding generation (sentence-transformers all-MiniLM-L6-v2) → Qdrant per-tenant collections
Retrieval query at chat time (top-k similarity search)
Context injection into LLM system prompt

steps.name: Frontend — Chat UI & Dashboard
steps.description: Next.js auth flow, protected layout shell, lead management, analytics dashboard, real-time WebSocket chat UI, and internal admin panel.

subTasks.name: Next.js 16 + TypeScript strict mode project scaffold
Tailwind CSS v4 + shadcn/ui component library with brand token system
API client with JWT refresh and localStorage token management
Authentication pages — login with UUID auto-format, form validation, password toggle
Protected route middleware (Next.js Edge Runtime, redirects to /login)
Layout shell — sidebar, mobile overlay, role-aware nav, logout
Lead management — list with filters/pagination, detail page, create dialog
Real-time chat UI — WebSocket client, streaming bubbles, TypingIndicator, BANT score card
Analytics dashboard — stats cards, recent leads, BANT reference card
Internal admin panel — /admin route group with overview (service health) and AI sandbox pages
Admin conversations list with per-conversation chat (shared ConversationChat component)

phase2.name: Multi-Tenant Dashboard & Analytics
phase2.description: Full SaaS dashboard, tenant management, usage analytics, and team collaboration features.

steps.name: Tenant Admin Panel
steps.description: Tenant signup, config management (AI persona, CORS, prompt editing), internal config endpoint for AI Worker, slug-based login.

subTasks.name: POST /api/v1/tenants/signup — public self-registration of tenant + owner
GET /api/v1/tenants/config — tenant config fetch (authenticated)
PATCH /api/v1/tenants/config — partial config update (ConfigManage permission)
GET /api/v1/internal/tenants/config — internal JWT endpoint for AI Worker
Assistant identity field (SDR name, persona) in tenant config
Frontend settings page — tenant config form with prompt editor
Slug-based workspace login (accepts slug or UUID)
Per-tenant CORS allowed origins config wired to AI Worker
Email verification on tenant signup
subTasks.notes: NEW-L-8 open finding — not yet implemented

steps.name: Analytics Engine
steps.description: Lead and conversation aggregate stats, token usage tracking by tenant and model, admin token-usage view.

subTasks.name: GET /api/v1/dashboard/stats — lead counts by status, total conversations, conversion rate
GET /api/v1/dashboard/conversations/stats — conversation aggregates
GET /api/v1/leads/recent — recent lead list for dashboard widget
Token usage recording on every AI Worker call (model, tokens, latency)
GET /api/v1/admin/token-usage — daily aggregated cost view (owner-scoped)
Frontend dashboard page with stats cards and recent leads table
Frontend admin token-usage page with chart
Time-range filters on token-usage endpoint
subTasks.notes: Basic date filtering exists; advanced range not implemented

subTasks.name: Cross-tenant super-admin analytics view

steps.name: Team & Role Management
steps.description: User invitation flow, role assignment, user listing and removal, invitation acceptance.

subTasks.name: POST /api/v1/users/invite — invite user by email with role assignment (UsersManage)
POST /api/v1/users/accept — accept invitation with token, creates user account
GET /api/v1/users — list all users in tenant
DELETE /api/v1/users/:id — remove user (RequireOwner only, with self-delete guard)
Phase 2 roles (RoleOwner, RoleMember, RoleReadOnly) defined and functional
Frontend invite-user dialog component with role selector
Frontend users page with member list and invite button
Pagination on GET /api/v1/users (unbounded list — NEW-L-3 open finding)
Role change endpoint (no PATCH /users/:id/role implemented)

steps.name: Billing & Usage Limits
steps.description: Token usage tracking per tenant/model, cost aggregation, basic usage caps.

subTasks.name: TokenUsage domain entity and repository with AggregateByDay
Token usage recorded on every LLM call via AI Worker client
Admin endpoint for owner to view own-tenant usage costs
Configurable per-tenant token quota / hard cap enforcement
Billing alert when usage exceeds threshold
Usage export (CSV or API)

steps.name: Notifications & Alerts
steps.description: Email or in-app notifications for key events (new lead, handoff, quota warning).

phase3.name: CRM Integrations & Channel Expansion
phase3.description: Native integrations with RD Station, HubSpot, Pipefy, WhatsApp Business, and Slack notifications.

phase4.name: Enterprise Features & Fine-Tuning
phase4.description: Dedicated AI worker pools, custom model fine-tuning, advanced RBAC, SLA monitoring, and Kubernetes deployment.

positiveFindings: All repository queries use GORM parameterized placeholders — no SQL injection surface
Every query is scoped with AND tenant_id = ? — cross-tenant access blocked at DB layer
No hardcoded secrets anywhere — all config via env vars
Domain structs carry no framework imports — hexagonal isolation is real
SQL migrations are embedded in the binary — no runtime file tampering possible
Graceful shutdown with signal handling correctly implemented
Foreign key constraints with ON DELETE CASCADE enforce referential integrity
JTI claim added to all tokens for revocation support; logout blacklists JTI in Redis
Refresh token rotation implemented — new pair issued on every /auth/refresh call
Security response headers enforced on all routes (X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, HSTS, Referrer-Policy)
CORS configured with explicit allowed origins via gin-contrib/cors
Permission-based RBAC (LeadsRead/Write, ConversationsRead/Write, UsersManage, ConfigManage, RAGManage) on every protected route
AI Worker HTTP client has explicit configurable timeout
Token bucket rate limiter with per-IP isolation verified in integration tests
Request-ID middleware enables full request tracing across distributed logs
Open self-registration endpoint (NEW-C-1) removed — onboarding exclusively via invitation flow
Document repository now fully tenant-isolated — FindAll/FindByID/Delete/UpdateStatus all scope by tenant_id (NEW-H-1 fixed)
Phase 2 roles (RoleOwner, RoleMember, RoleReadOnly) accepted by jwtutil.Parse() — owner/member/readonly users can authenticate (NEW-H-2 fixed)
DELETE /users/:id upgraded to RequireOwner(); self-delete and owner-delete guards added in use case (NEW-H-3 fixed)
WebSocket endpoint now behind per-IP rate limiter (3 RPS, burst 10) with 5-connection cap per conversation (NEW-H-4 fixed)
Conversation list page cap (maxPage = 1000) mirrors leads pattern — deep-offset DoS prevented (NEW-H-5 fixed)
Cross-tenant token-usage view removed from owner JWT flow; endpoint now strictly tenant-scoped (NEW-H-6 fixed)
git pre-commit hook runs unit tests before commit; pre-push hook runs integration tests before push

audits.name: Maestro Core Security Audit — Round 1
audits.scope: Go backend — 23 source files, Steps 1 & 2 (domain, repositories, middleware, server)

findings.title: JWT_SECRET not validated on startup
Status filter value not validated before query
IDOR — missing tenant isolation in FindByConversationID
Cross-tenant message injection via IDOR on Insert
HTTP server has no read/write/idle timeouts (Slowloris)
No size limit on Message.Content
No size limit on Lead.Metadata / Tenant.Config JSONB
Health endpoint leaks environment name
No rate limiting or body-size cap on any route
IP spoofing / rate limit bypass via X-Forwarded-For
Mutex held for full map iteration in cleanupLoop
Mass assignment / tenant injection in Create
Cross-tenant IDOR via unvalidated LeadID on Conversation
Global write lock held during Allow() slow path
Mass assignment / privilege escalation in Lead Update
Unbounded message retrieval — memory exhaustion DoS
tenantIDFromContext returns empty string silently
AIWorkerURL and port not validated in config
gin.Logger() may log Authorization header values
Logger sync error silently ignored on shutdown
Unbounded memory growth in RateLimiter entries map
Incomplete domain validation (Role and ConversationStatus)
Case-sensitivity bypass in log redaction
Database DoS via unbounded offset pagination
DB connection pool missing ConnMaxLifetime / ConnMaxIdleTime
No startup retry when PostgreSQL is temporarily unavailable
No unique constraint on (tenant_id, email) for leads
No string length validation on Lead.Name, Email, Phone

audits.name: Maestro Core Security Audit — Round 2
audits.scope: Go backend — HTTP handlers, auth use case, JWT, config, middleware (Steps 3–7 additions)

findings.title: No RBAC on tenant creation — any authenticated user could create tenants
No security headers (X-Frame-Options, HSTS, etc.)
No JTI claim — token revocation impossible
No CORS configuration — open to cross-origin requests
No refresh token rotation — stolen refresh token reusable indefinitely
Role value not validated in JWT parse — arbitrary role injection
Hardcoded bcrypt demo password in migration file
Field-level RBAC missing on PATCH /leads/:id — agents could escalate lead status
Service token lacks JTI — replay risk
Rate limiter bypassable via spoofed client IP (TRUSTED_PROXIES too wide)
Missing audit logging for sensitive auth operations
APP_ENV not validated — unexpected logging modes in prod
No UUID validation on path parameters
Request binding tags missing max-length constraints on lead fields
Demo credentials documented in plaintext in source (migration comment)
Response pagination page cap is silent — client confusion risk
DB connection pool has no explicit acquisition timeout
No explicit HTTP client for AI Worker — inherits Go default (no timeout)
Metadata JSONB validates after full parse — minor DoS surface
Unbounded memory allocation for conversation history — 500 msgs × 100 KB = 50 MB
Unhandled tenant domain collision and lack of slug normalization

audits.name: Maestro Core Security Audit — Round 3
audits.scope: Full source audit — all Go packages in maestro/ (Phase 2 additions: RAG pipeline, user management, invitation flow, token usage, tenant config, WebSocket Phase 2)

findings.title: Open self-registration to any tenant — POST /auth/register public with no invitation
findings.fixedIn: fix: resolve 6 high-severity findings from security audit #3

findings.title: Document repository missing tenant isolation (IDOR) — FindAll/FindByID/Delete unscoped
findings.fixedIn: fix: resolve 6 high-severity findings from security audit #3

findings.title: JWT Parse rejects Phase 2 roles — DoS on Owner/Member/ReadOnly users
findings.fixedIn: fix: resolve 6 high-severity findings from security audit #3

findings.title: Admin can delete the tenant owner — wrong guard (RequirePermission vs RequireOwner)
findings.fixedIn: fix: resolve 6 high-severity findings from security audit #3

findings.title: WebSocket endpoint bypasses rate limiter — unlimited connections and AI calls
findings.fixedIn: fix: resolve 6 high-severity findings from security audit #3

findings.title: No page cap on conversation list — deep-offset DoS
findings.fixedIn: fix: resolve 6 high-severity findings from security audit #3

findings.title: Owner role grants cross-tenant token usage access — empty tenant_id returns all-tenant data
findings.fixedIn: fix: resolve 6 high-severity findings from security audit #3

findings.title: Redis credentials (full DSN with password) logged in plaintext at INFO level on startup
SetTenantContext uses session-level SET — tenant_id bleeds across connection pool when RLS is restrictive
Field-level RBAC missing on PATCH /leads/:id (re-confirmed from Audit #2 — fix was incomplete)
No status validation on GET /conversations — invalid status passes to GORM WHERE clause
No UUID validation on GET /conversations/:id — arbitrary strings traverse full stack
Token revocation fail-open on Redis error — transient Redis outage restores revoked tokens
Invitation token stored as plaintext in DB — DB breach enables invite takeover
RAG background goroutines have no timeout — goroutine accumulation DoS under slow AI Worker
WebSocket has no per-message size limit — 500 MB frame exhausts server RAM
Batch sandbox delete has no array size cap — large IN (...) query DoS against DB
No UUID validation on RAG document endpoints
No per-conversation WebSocket connection cap (pre-fix; tracked here for completeness)
findings.fixedIn: fix: resolve 6 high-severity findings from security audit #3

findings.title: Old refresh token never invalidated after rotation — 7-day window for stolen token reuse
Silent Redis startup failure permanently disables token revocation — health check returns OK
TenantRepository.Update missing caller-tenant authorization — cross-tenant mutation possible
AggregateByDay no tenant guard at repo layer — empty tenantID returns all-tenant financial data
Logout blacklist write silently discarded (nolint:errcheck) — token stays live 1 hour on Redis failure
AuthUseCase lacks ISessionStore field — refresh token blacklisting structurally impossible
RAG DeleteDocument cross-tenant file deletion — unscoped FindByID deletes wrong tenant's MinIO object
WebSocket chat handler missing ConversationsWrite permission check
Single HMAC secret shared across all JWT trust domains — blast radius covers user and service tokens
Access token accepted as refresh token — 1-hour token upgrades to 7-day session
Server uptime leaked in /health response to unauthenticated callers
Prometheus /metrics endpoint unauthenticated — exposes tenant UUIDs and traffic patterns
User list GET /users has no pagination or count limit
Invitation role not validated against valid enum — arbitrary role string stored in DB
No per-lead conversation count limit — unlimited conversation creation per lead
SSE scanner default buffer (64 KB) smaller than max message content (100 KB) — stream error risk
InternalJWTMiddleware does not inject tenant_id into request context
No email verification on tenant signup — any email address usable
AI Worker retry client retries non-retryable errors (401/400) — 7-second delay on auth failures
Missing Content-Security-Policy response header
tenant_id always empty in access logs and Prometheus metrics — TenantMiddleware uses request context not Gin context
User-supplied X-Request-ID accepted without validation — log injection via newlines/ANSI sequences
Consumed invitation token silently persists in DB if delete fails — potential reuse window
WebSocket conversation_id path parameter not validated as UUID
HSTS header set unconditionally — applies to HTTP-only development environments
Tenant slug enumerable via distinct HTTP response codes on login (400 vs 401)
MinIO SSL disabled by default — RAG files transmitted unencrypted on internal network
GET /health/worker unauthenticated — exposes AI Worker health state including model names
AuthUseCase.Register permits RoleOwner at domain layer — HTTP handler bypass can create owner accounts
WebSocket has no ping/pong keep-alive — idle/zombie connections held indefinitely
Token usage date range unbounded — owners can trigger full-table scan with 1970–2099 range query
No per-endpoint authentication rate limiting — password spraying at 10 RPS unconstrained

patterns: JWT with JTI and Redis Blacklist for token revocation
WebSocket Real-time Bridge (Hub pattern with per-conversation connection cap)
Cache-aside Pattern (Redis conversation history, tenant config)
Testcontainers for ephemeral integration test infrastructure
Static RAG with FAISS (file-based vector retrieval + context injection)
Per-tenant Qdrant vector collections for dynamic RAG (Phase 2)
Token usage telemetry — fire-and-forget recording on every LLM call
Internal JWT trust domain — service tokens for Maestro → AI Worker auth
git hooks enforcing test gates on commit (unit) and push (integration)

shipped: git pre-commit hook: runs `make test` (unit tests) before every commit — prevents broken commits from reaching main
git pre-push hook: runs `make test-integration` before every push — prevents broken integration tests from being pushed
fixed: NEW-C-1 (Critical): Removed POST /api/v1/auth/register public endpoint — onboarding now exclusively via invitation flow; self-registration to any tenant no longer possible

NEW-H-1 (High): DocumentRepository now fully tenant-isolated — FindByID, FindAll, Delete, and UpdateStatus all apply AND tenant_id = ? guard; RAG goroutines inject tenant context via ctxkeys.WithTenantID
NEW-H-2 (High): jwtutil.Parse() extended to accept RoleOwner, RoleMember, RoleReadOnly — owner/member/readonly users can now authenticate without receiving 401
NEW-H-3 (High): DELETE /users/:id upgraded to RequireOwner() guard; UserUseCase.RemoveUser adds callerID/targetID check preventing self-deletion and owner account deletion by admins
NEW-H-4 (High): WebSocket endpoint now behind dedicated RateLimiter (3 RPS, burst 10 per IP); Hub.Register returns error when conversation reaches 5 concurrent connections
NEW-H-5 (High): Conversation list page cap maxPage=1000 added to ConversationRepository — mirrors leads pattern; deep-offset PostgreSQL table scan prevented
NEW-H-6 (High): Cross-tenant token-usage view removed from owner JWT flow; GET /admin/token-usage now strictly tenant-scoped; AggregateByDay caller cannot pass empty tenantID
blockers: 44 open findings from Audit #3 (2 high, 20 medium, 22 low) — require prioritised remediation before production launch
ADD-M-5: AuthUseCase lacks ISessionStore — refresh token blacklisting requires structural refactor before it can be implemented
F-08: Field-level RBAC on PATCH /leads/:id still allows member-role users to override AI-assigned lead status — confirmed in Audit #3 as not fixed despite Audit #2 claim
nextWeekPlanned: Fix ADD-M-7: Add ConversationsWrite permission check to WebSocket chat handler (currently read-only users can inject AI messages)
Fix ADD-M-9: Add token_type claim to distinguish access vs refresh tokens in jwtutil.Issue/Parse
Fix ADD-M-5 + NEW-M-10: Inject ISessionStore into AuthUseCase; blacklist old refresh token JTI on rotation
Fix NEW-M-3 + ADD-M-4: Make blacklist check and write fail-closed on Redis errors
Fix F-08: Add per-field role check in PATCH /leads/:id handler — gate status override behind ConfigManage or ExportData permission
